service: Assist and educate businesses about their contributions towards a fair marketplace for all.
oneLiner: This work matters because many businesses violate consumer and licensing rules out of ignorance rather than intent, so DCWP runs free compliance visits and a legal helpline to help owners follow the rules before they're fined, not just punish them after.
summary: DCWP's Visiting Inspector Program offers new and existing brick-and-mortar businesses a free, educational visit from a senior inspector to explain relevant regulations and help them avoid violations, rather than issuing citations. The agency also operates a Business Compliance Counsel and a Legal Compliance Helpline so business owners can get direct answers about the consumer protection and licensing laws DCWP enforces. This educational approach reflects the agency's broader mission, dating to the 1969 Consumer Protection Law that created its predecessor, the Department of Consumer Affairs, to foster a fair marketplace for both consumers and honest businesses.

sources.label: DCWP: Resolve DCWP Issues (Visiting Inspector Program)
